# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-a-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-a
  port: 80

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-a-authz
  namespace: default
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: ""
    kind: Service
    name: service-a
  server:
    podSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: service-a
    port: 80
  rules:
  - from:
    - network: 192.168.1.0/24
    - serviceAccount:
        namespaces: ["default"]
        names: ["service-b", "service-c"]

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-b-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-b
  port: 80

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-b-authz
  namespace: default
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: ""
    kind: Service
    name: service-b
  server:
    podSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: service-b
    port: 80
  rules:
  - from:
    - serviceAccount:
        namespaces: ["default"]
        names: ["service-a"]

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: service-c-server
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: service-c
  port: 80

---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: ServerAuthorization
metadata:
  name: service-c-authz
  namespace: default
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: ""
    kind: Service
    name: service-c
  server:
    podSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: service-c
    port: 80
  rules:
  - from:
    - network: 192.168.1.0/24
